repo_notes.content: MayorSystem is a production Paper/Kotlin Minecraft plugin for scheduled mayor elections, candidates, voting, elected mayors, perks, displays, admin tools, storage, optional integrations, and addon development. Treat docs/ as the human-written documentation source and src/main/kotlin as the implementation source. Keep the wiki factual and source-grounded.

Public addon API is intentionally limited to mayorSystem.api, mayorSystem.api.snapshot, and mayorSystem.api.events. Do not present MayorPlugin, services, storage models, command classes, platform runtime classes, GUI classes, or config sections as supported public API.

The plugin uses clear architecture boundaries: platform.paper owns Bukkit/Paper lifecycle, application/usecase owns workflows, domain owns pure election/perk rules, data owns storage, integration owns optional plugin adapters, ui owns inventory menus, and config owns typed configuration/default sync behavior.
When documenting commands, use /mayor examples. The plugin and command titles are configurable, but /mayor is the canonical docs command.

Important compatibility promises: existing commands, permissions, config keys, storage schema, public events, PlaceholderAPI placeholders, optional integration behavior, and server-owner config edits must remain stable unless a future release explicitly documents a migration.

pages.title: MayorSystem Overview
pages.purpose: Explain what MayorSystem does, who it is for, the main gameplay systems, supported server requirements, and where the repository docs, downloads, and DeepWiki comparison entrypoints live.

pages.title: Runtime Architecture
pages.purpose: Document the MayorPlugin to MayorRuntime lifecycle, service container, enable phases, ready/loading state, async bootstrap, reload flow, disable order, scheduler/thread boundaries, and API service registration.

pages.title: Election Domain
pages.purpose: Document term calculation, election windows, pause/override behavior, candidate eligibility, vote policy, mayor selection, fake votes, force election paths, stepdown behavior, and related tests.

pages.title: Player Workflows
pages.purpose: Document the player-facing /mayor flows: status, apply, vote, candidate menus, perk selection, custom requests, stepdown, GUI rendering, and permission gates.

pages.title: Admin Workflows
pages.purpose: Document admin command families and use cases: system toggles, settings, elections, candidates, perks, display rewards, NPC/hologram management, monitoring, maintenance, reloads, audit logging, and failure behavior.

pages.title: Perk System
pages.purpose: Document perk sections, definitions, pick limits, active perk rebuild behavior, custom perk requests, bonus terms, addon-provided perk sections, config seeding, and tests around perk safety.

pages.title: Display Rewards
pages.purpose: Document LuckPerms rank rewards, DeluxeTags tag rewards, track/group/user targets, default mode, target resolution, health checks, sync behavior, failure handling, and user-value-preserving config writes.

pages.title: Storage And Data Safety
pages.purpose: Document MayorStore, ElectionRepository, SQLite/MySQL implementations, schema/migration behavior, startup fail-closed behavior, reset behavior, shutdown behavior, and storage-related tests.

pages.title: Configuration
pages.purpose: Document config.yml, messages.yml, gui.yml, default sync, non-destructive migrations, validation rules, title/name customization, reload behavior, and server-owner contract guarantees.

pages.title: Optional Integrations
pages.purpose: Document Vault, LuckPerms, PlaceholderAPI, Citizens, FancyNpcs, DecentHolograms, FancyHolograms, SystemSellAddon, SystemSkyblockStyleAddon, Noop fallbacks, adapter boundaries, and plugin-disable cleanup behavior.

pages.title: Public Addon API
pages.purpose: Document the new MayorSystemApi entrypoint, immutable snapshots, public events, MayorAddonRegistration, MayorPerkSource, MayorPerkSection, MayorPerkDefinition, ServicesManager lookup, API jar usage, and forbidden internal dependencies.

pages.title: Addon Perk Sources
pages.purpose: Document addon perk-source registration, id validation, duplicate handling, owner disable cleanup, unavailable source behavior, missing-only config seeding, reload-on-change behavior, and examples for addon developers.

pages.title: Commands And Permissions
pages.purpose: Document the /mayor command tree, command registration, Cloud permissions, permission node defaults, tab completion expectations, console/player behavior, and how commands delegate into use cases.

pages.title: Menus And UI
pages.purpose: Document player/admin inventory menus, menu access rules, chat prompts, display text rendering, GUI config behavior, skull/profile handling, and UX boundaries between UI and application services.

pages.title: Testing And Quality Gates
pages.purpose: Document the unit/integration test coverage by subsystem, build commands, API jar verification, legacy ABI checks, source guard tests, local Paper server smoke tests, and log-scan expectations.

pages.title: Release And Documentation
pages.purpose: Document the repository docs, DeepWiki steering, generated release data, Maven Central API package expectations, README scope, and where server-owner versus addon-developer documentation lives.
